bmw:m3.overview: The BMW M3 is one of the benchmark enthusiast nameplates of the modern era, spanning lightweight homologation roots, naturally aspirated icons, and later turbocharged performance cars. Values tend to move sharply based on generation, transmission, originality, maintenance history, and whether the car sits closer to collector-grade or daily-driver territory.

quick_facts.Known for: Balanced chassis, motorsport lineage, high-spec driver cars
quick_facts.Collector focus: E30, E36, E46, E92, and limited-run CSL/GTS/CRT style variants

highlights: Generation changes matter a lot because the market treats each era almost like a different product.
Manual transmission and low-mile, unmodified examples usually command the strongest premiums.
Documentation around rod bearings, subframe work, cooling systems, and major services can move confidence quickly.
common_questions: Which M3 generation am I actually comparing this car against?
How original is the car versus modified-for-track or taste?
What known maintenance items have already been addressed?
buying_tips: Do not comp different generations together unless you are intentionally zooming way out.
Look closely at service documentation for generation-specific failure points.
Original paint, factory equipment, and tasteful modifications tend to price very differently.

ford:bronco.overview: The Ford Bronco spans multiple very different eras, from compact early SUVs to full-size trucks and the modern revival. That makes year, body style, driveline, and build quality especially important when trying to price one accurately.

quick_facts.Known for: Body-on-frame utility, removable-top variants, broad enthusiast aftermarket
quick_facts.Collector focus: Early Broncos, special editions, well-restored uncut trucks, clean modern halo builds

highlights: The market treats early Broncos, full-size Broncos, and the modern reboot as separate worlds.
Rust, body work quality, chassis originality, and drivetrain swaps can change value dramatically.
A clean uncut early truck with documentation often prices very differently from a modified trail build.
common_questions: Is this an early Bronco, a full-size Bronco, or the modern reboot?
How much sheet metal, chassis, and drivetrain work is original versus replaced?
Was the truck restored for road use, off-road use, or cosmetics?
buying_tips: Prioritize rust inspection and restoration quality before comparing sticker prices.
Year and body configuration matter enough that exact-year comping is safer than broad model comping.
Modifications can add appeal for one buyer and destroy originality for another, so read the market carefully.

ford:mustang.overview: The Ford Mustang market is wide and fast-moving, with early cars, Fox bodies, SVT-era cars, Shelby variants, and modern performance trims behaving like separate sub-markets. Year, engine, special-edition packaging, and originality matter a lot more here than the shared Mustang badge suggests.

quick_facts.Known for: Accessible performance, huge aftermarket, deep special-edition and Shelby ecosystem
quick_facts.Collector focus: K-code and big-block classics, Boss/Shelby cars, Cobra R, Terminator, GT350, and GT500 variants

highlights: The market treats standard Mustangs, SVT cars, and Shelby variants very differently even when they are close in age.
Documentation around engine originality, factory options, and major upgrades moves value quickly.
Special-edition cues that sound minor on paper can matter a lot once buyers start comping rare trims.
common_questions: Is this a mainstream Mustang trim or a genuinely special variant?
How much of the drivetrain, body, and paint are still factory-correct?
Are the current mods helping buyer demand or narrowing the audience?
buying_tips: Do not lump Shelby, SVT, and standard GT cars into the same comp set.
Check build sheets, VIN decoders, and documentation before assuming a trim badge is original.
On modified cars, focus on whether the work improves the spec buyers actually pay up for.

land-rover:defender.overview: The Land Rover Defender market splits sharply between true classic trucks, NAS imports, restored restomods, and the modern reboot. Specification, legal import story, rust or corrosion exposure, and restoration quality all matter heavily.

quick_facts.Known for: Boxy off-road utility, global-spec variation, strong restoration and overlanding demand
quick_facts.Collector focus: NAS trucks, original station wagons, clean 90/110 examples, and high-quality restorations

highlights: Imported Defenders can look similar while carrying very different drivetrains, specs, and legality stories.
Rust, galvanic corrosion, and restoration quality can matter more than odometer readings.
Modern Defenders attract a different buyer than classic ladder-frame trucks and should not be blended casually.
common_questions: Is this a classic Defender, a NAS truck, or the modern reboot?
How original is the chassis and drivetrain versus rebuilt or swapped?
Was the truck restored for serious use, visual impact, or both?
buying_tips: Prioritize chassis condition and documentation over cosmetic freshness.
Be careful with imported trucks whose paperwork or spec story is thin.
Comp classic and modern Defenders separately even when the badges match.

land-rover:range-rover.overview: Range Rover values depend on which era you are looking at, because early two-door trucks, P38/L322 luxury SUVs, and newer halo-spec models live in very different markets. Condition, electronics, deferred maintenance, and the exact trim or engine package matter a lot.

quick_facts.Known for: Luxury off-road positioning, broad drivetrain mix, and high maintenance sensitivity
quick_facts.Collector focus: Early two-door and classic trucks, special-performance trims, and unusually clean later examples

highlights: Classic Range Rovers and later luxury SUVs appeal to different buyers and should be valued on separate curves.
Maintenance history and electrical health can swing buyer confidence faster than mileage alone.
Options and trim packages matter because buyers in this market often shop for a very specific spec.
common_questions: Which Range Rover generation am I actually comparing against?
How much deferred maintenance could be hiding behind the current price?
Is the value driver luxury spec, off-road credibility, or collector-era originality?
buying_tips: Use generation-aware comps before looking at headline pricing.
Treat service history and known electrical repairs as first-class pricing inputs.
Special trims can deserve premiums, but only if the condition supports them.

mazda:mx-5-miata.overview: The Mazda MX-5 Miata market is broad but very trim- and condition-sensitive, with clean unmodified cars pricing differently from track-prepped builds, turbo cars, and later RF or special-edition examples. Small changes in originality can matter more than people expect because the buyer base is so informed.

quick_facts.Known for: Lightweight rear-drive handling, huge enthusiast base, approachable maintenance
quick_facts.Collector focus: NA/NB special editions, Mazdaspeed cars, low-mile stock survivors, and Club/RF specs

highlights: The market rewards clean stock cars differently from modified autocross or track builds.
Limited editions and factory performance packages tend to matter more than generic cosmetic upgrades.
Later RF cars and early NA cars often attract very different buyers.
common_questions: Is this car still close to stock or aimed at track-day buyers?
Which generation and special package am I really comparing here?
Has rust or deferred maintenance started to affect the floor price?
buying_tips: Split stock, boosted, and heavily track-focused cars into different comp sets.
Look closely at rust, top condition, and maintenance before paying for low miles.
Special editions deserve proof, because badge-only claims are common.

acura:integra.overview: The Acura Integra market splits between honest driver-grade cars, lightweight GS-R and Type R halo variants, and modified tuner-era builds. Buyers care a lot about trim authenticity, transmission, rust, and whether the car still feels period-correct rather than pieced together.

quick_facts.Known for: Rev-happy front-drive chassis, tuner credibility, lightweight packaging
quick_facts.Collector focus: GS-R, Type R, and unusually clean low-mile original survivors

highlights: Trim accuracy matters because LS, GS-R, and Type R buyers are shopping very different cars.
Rust, theft history, and swapped engines can change confidence faster than cosmetic presentation.
Clean manuals with strong documentation tend to command the best attention.
common_questions: Is this a real higher-spec trim or a cosmetic clone?
How original is the drivetrain and ECU setup?
Does the current condition support collector-grade pricing or just enthusiast-driver pricing?
buying_tips: Verify VIN and trim documentation before trusting badges.
Rust inspection and originality usually matter more than small mileage differences.
Tasteful period modifications trade differently from rough tuner-era builds.

mercedes-benz:e-class.overview: The Mercedes-Benz E-Class spans elegant coupes and cabriolets, durable wagons, diesel commuters, and AMG performance cars, so broad E-Class pricing is rarely enough on its own. Buyers care about body style, drivetrain, service history, and whether the car presents as a collector cruiser or a deferred-maintenance project.

quick_facts.Known for: Long-lived executive-car platform, broad drivetrain mix, durable wagon and diesel reputation
quick_facts.Collector focus: AMG cars, cabriolets, wagons, diesel oddballs, and unusually clean analog-era examples

highlights: Body style matters a lot because wagons, cabriolets, and sedans live on different price curves.
Diesel and AMG badges can materially change the buyer pool even within the same generation.
Maintenance depth often matters more than low advertised mileage.
common_questions: Is this E-Class interesting because of body style, drivetrain, or condition?
What generation-specific maintenance has already been handled?
Is the market reading this as a usable classic, a specialty trim, or a project?
buying_tips: Separate wagons, cabriolets, diesels, and AMG cars before comping.
Look for documentation on suspension, cooling, electronics, and drivetrain upkeep.
A clean boring-looking E-Class often trades better than a flashier neglected one.

nissan:300zx.overview: The Nissan 300ZX market varies a lot by generation, body style, and drivetrain, especially once turbocharged Z32 cars enter the picture. Buyers pay close attention to maintenance history, originality, and whether the car is an honest grand-touring driver or a heavily modified nostalgia play.

quick_facts.Known for: Z-car heritage, twin-turbo halo variants, dramatic 1990s styling
quick_facts.Collector focus: Z32 Twin Turbo manuals, low-mile original survivors, and special body configurations

highlights: Turbocharged Z cars behave like a different market from naturally aspirated 2+2 examples.
Deferred maintenance can hide behind attractive pricing because engine-bay work is time-intensive.
Originality and tasteful preservation matter more than giant mod lists in the current collector market.
common_questions: Is this the spec enthusiasts actually chase, or a broader GT version?
How much major maintenance has already been handled recently?
Does the car lean collector-grade, driver-grade, or tuner-project?
buying_tips: Separate Turbo, Twin Turbo, and 2+2 cars before drawing price conclusions.
Cooling, timing-belt, fuel-system, and hose documentation should move your confidence fast.
Heavily modified cars can be fun, but they do not always trade with the same buyer pool.

tesla:model-s.overview: The Tesla Model S market is unusually software- and battery-sensitive, with values shaped by pack size, performance spec, charging hardware, screen/computer generation, and warranty or service history. Buyers often look past the badge and shop very specific combinations of battery, age, and feature set.

quick_facts.Known for: Long-range EV packaging, software-defined ownership experience, early premium-EV halo status
quick_facts.Collector focus: Performance trims, unusually low-mile early cars, and clean documented battery stories

highlights: Battery spec matters enough that a Model S badge alone is not a meaningful comp bucket.
Charging hardware, MCU generation, and warranty/service history can move value quickly.
A lower-mile early car is not always more desirable than a later better-supported spec.
common_questions: What battery and drivetrain configuration is this car actually using?
How much confidence is there around battery health and major service history?
Is the market treating this as a cheap luxury EV, a performance flagship, or a tech curiosity?
buying_tips: Separate battery sizes and performance trims before comparing prices.
Documentation around battery, suspension, and MCU work matters a lot.
Software-era features can matter almost as much as cosmetics on older Teslas.

volkswagen:touareg.overview: The Volkswagen Touareg market is spec-sensitive because diesel, V8, and later luxury-oriented examples can feel like very different vehicles to buyers. Condition, maintenance complexity, and drivetrain choice matter more than the shared Touareg badge suggests.

quick_facts.Known for: Overbuilt shared-platform SUV engineering, broad engine spread, understated luxury
quick_facts.Collector focus: TDI cars, V8 oddballs, low-mile high-spec examples, and unusual option combinations

highlights: The drivetrain is often the biggest value driver because buyers shop TDI, VR6, and V8 cars differently.
Maintenance complexity can outweigh apparent bargain pricing quickly.
Well-kept Touaregs attract a narrower but knowledgeable buyer pool.
common_questions: Is the appeal here fuel economy, oddball engine spec, or luxury value?
How deep is the service history on suspension, cooling, and drivetrain systems?
Does the current price reflect rarity, condition, or just low demand?
buying_tips: Do not mix TDI and V8 cars without a deliberate adjustment.
Complexity and service depth matter more here than headline mileage.
A very clean documented Touareg can deserve a premium over a cheaper neglected one.

volvo:v60.overview: The Volvo V60 market ranges from sensible wagons to genuinely quick electrified or performance-oriented trims, so the drivetrain and package name matter more than the shared wagon silhouette. Buyers tend to value rarity, equipment, and ownership story alongside outright condition.

quick_facts.Known for: Wagon practicality, understated design, unusual enthusiast appeal in higher-spec trims
quick_facts.Collector focus: Polestar-related cars, Recharge variants, and rare well-optioned wagons

highlights: The market distinguishes clearly between ordinary V60s and the rarer enthusiast or hybrid halo specs.
Spec and equipment can move value more than buyers expect in this niche wagon segment.
Documented maintenance and a clean cosmetic story matter because the buyer pool is small but picky.
common_questions: Is this a mainstream V60 or a genuinely special drivetrain/package combination?
How rare is this exact spec in the local market?
Does the current ask reflect rarity, upkeep, or optimism?
buying_tips: Separate T6, Recharge, and Polestar-flavored cars before comping.
Look at spec sheet, options, and ownership history alongside mileage.
Rare wagons can deserve patience, but only if the condition story is strong.

porsche:cayenne.overview: The Porsche Cayenne market ranges from practical daily SUVs to serious performance variants, so trims like S, GTS, and Turbo can sit on very different price curves. Service history, air-suspension health, cooling issues, transfer-case history, and the exact drivetrain spec all matter.

quick_facts.Known for: Performance-SUV tuning, broad engine spread, and high-spec daily usability
quick_facts.Collector focus: Turbo, Turbo S, GTS, transaxle-era special builds, and unusually clean low-mile examples

highlights: The performance trims behave like separate products, so broad Cayenne comps can hide real value differences.
Deferred maintenance matters a lot because repair complexity climbs quickly on higher-spec cars.
Mileage alone is not enough; option mix and upkeep often matter more.
common_questions: Is this a base-family Cayenne or a premium performance trim?
How complete is the service story around air suspension, cooling, and driveline systems?
Does the asking price reflect upkeep, spec, or just the badge?
buying_tips: Separate S, GTS, Turbo, and hybrid cars before comping.
Use maintenance depth and option content alongside mileage when judging value.
A cheap high-spec Cayenne can be far more expensive than a pricier well-kept one.

porsche:macan.overview: The Porsche Macan market is trim-driven, with S, GTS, Turbo, and later four-cylinder or EV-adjacent positioning creating different buyer pools. The best comparables depend on trim, option content, maintenance, and whether the car presents as a daily driver or a premium performance crossover.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact luxury crossover packaging with unusually sporty chassis tuning
quick_facts.Collector focus: Turbo and GTS trims, rare specs, and very clean enthusiast-owned examples

highlights: Macan buyers often shop specific trims, so the model family alone is not enough to price accurately.
The difference between a well-optioned driver and a halo-spec Turbo can be material.
Local market demand is often strongest for highly usable specs with strong service history.
common_questions: Which Macan trim am I actually evaluating here?
How much of the price is trim-driven versus mileage and options?
Does the car read as enthusiast-owned or just well-presented?
buying_tips: Do not mix base, S, GTS, and Turbo cars without a deliberate trim adjustment.
Pay attention to option packages because buyers in this segment shop equipment closely.
Service depth and clean ownership stories can be worth more than another few thousand miles.

subaru:impreza.overview: The Subaru Impreza name covers ordinary commuter cars, homologation-flavored WRX and STI variants, and import-market specials, so year and trim matter enormously. The value gap between a base Impreza and a real WRX STI can be massive even when the bodies look related.

quick_facts.Known for: Turbo AWD performance variants, rally heritage, and strong enthusiast tuning culture
quick_facts.Collector focus: 22B-style halo cars, clean WRX STI examples, wagon variants, and low-mile originals

highlights: Impreza pricing is extremely trim-sensitive because enthusiasts shop WRX and STI cars as their own market.
Modifications can be either a value drag or a selling point depending on execution and buyer type.
Documentation around engine, turbo, and drivetrain work matters more than the badge alone.
common_questions: Is this a normal Impreza, a WRX, or a true STI-spec car?
How original is the drivetrain versus tuned or rebuilt?
Does the car appeal to collectors, drivers, or project buyers?
buying_tips: Separate WRX and STI variants before comping anything.
Treat engine history, tune quality, and rust as first-class pricing inputs.
Imported or special-market cars need stronger documentation than normal U.S. examples.

porsche:911.overview: The Porsche 911 is one of the deepest collector-car markets in the world, with values shaped by generation, engine, body style, gearbox, provenance, and originality. Even within a single era, trim, mileage, and condition can materially change the right comp set.

quick_facts.Known for: Rear-engine layout, long production arc, deep enthusiast and collector demand
quick_facts.Collector focus: Air-cooled cars, homologation specials, Turbo models, RS/GT variants, low-mile original survivors

highlights: Generation and drivetrain changes are meaningful enough that broad 911 comps can mislead quickly.
Original paint, matching-numbers status, service history, and tasteful factory spec usually matter a lot.
Air-cooled and water-cooled markets behave differently and should rarely be blended.
common_questions: Which generation and sub-variant am I really comparing here?
How original is the car mechanically and cosmetically?
What maintenance or deferred work sits behind the current asking price?
buying_tips: Use generation-aware comps before anything else.
Treat documentation, leak-down/compression data, and major recent service as first-class pricing inputs.
Body style and gearbox can be large value separators even inside the same generation.

buick:roadmaster.overview: The Buick Roadmaster market splits between chrome-era cruisers and the later LT1-powered wagons and sedans that now have real enthusiast followings. Condition, originality, and whether the car leans preserved survivor, restomod, or tow-rig nostalgia build matter a lot.

quick_facts.Known for: Large American comfort, estate-wagon cult following, LT1 V8 oddball appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: Estate Wagons, Limited trims, woodgrain survivors, and clean LT1-powered cars

highlights: The 1990s wagons trade on a very different curve from older chrome-era Roadmasters.
Wagon condition, interior preservation, and whether the drivetrain is still stock affect demand quickly.
Tasteful upgrades can help usability, but heavy customization narrows the buyer pool.
common_questions: Is this Roadmaster interesting because of body style, drivetrain, or nostalgia value?
How original is the wagon or sedan versus modified or supercharged?
Does the condition support collector pricing or just novelty pricing?
buying_tips: Separate wagons from sedans before drawing any price conclusions.
Check body, trim, tailgate, and interior-condition details closely because replacement parts are not always easy.
On LT1 cars, cooling, OptiSpark-era service, and ownership history matter more than the badge alone.

chevrolet:corvette.overview: The Chevrolet Corvette market is broad enough that generation, body style, engine, and performance package matter almost as much as the Corvette name itself. Buyers shop C2/C3 nostalgia cars, later analog V8 cars, and Z06 or track-focused variants as distinct sub-markets.

quick_facts.Known for: American V8 performance, long production arc, strong special-variant demand
quick_facts.Collector focus: Split-window C2s, big-blocks, ZR1/Z06 cars, low-mile manuals, and documented survivor examples

highlights: Generation and performance package matter enough that a broad Corvette comp set can be misleading.
Z06, ZR1, and other halo variants appeal to much more focused buyers than ordinary coupes.
Original drivetrain, paint story, and documentation around hard use or track use move confidence quickly.
common_questions: Am I comping the right Corvette generation and performance tier?
How much of the value is tied to Z06 or other halo-spec hardware?
Does the car present as a preserved collector example, a driver, or a track-focused build?
buying_tips: Separate base cars, Z06s, and other high-performance trims before reading price bands.
Look closely at service history, accident history, and any signs of repeated track use.
Originality, mileage, and factory-equipment correctness often matter more than flashy mods.

fiat:500.overview: The Fiat 500 market spans tiny air-cooled classics, later retro city cars, and trim-specific enthusiast variants, so exact era and spec matter immediately. The smallest details around originality, restoration quality, and body integrity can change the right comp set fast.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact Italian packaging, charming design, city-car simplicity
quick_facts.Collector focus: Classic Nuova 500 variants, well-restored 500F/500L cars, and unusual enthusiast-spec modern examples

highlights: Classic 500s and the modern retro generation should not be blended into the same value bucket.
Restoration quality and rust repair can matter more than small differences in trim badges.
The buyer pool is often driven by charm, correctness, and presentation rather than outright performance.
common_questions: Is this the classic rear-engine 500 or the modern retro hatch?
How authentic is the restoration or specification story?
Does the current value reflect rarity, condition, or just novelty?
buying_tips: Comp classic 500F/500L/500R cars separately from modern 500s.
Rust, panel fit, and restoration documentation matter more than optimistic cosmetic presentation.
On classic cars, originality and mechanical simplicity usually beat over-restored flash.

ford:f-250.overview: The Ford F-250 market ranges from honest work trucks to collectible heavy-duty pickups with diesel, off-road, or period-correct appeal. Buyers care about cab style, engine choice, driveline, rust, and whether the truck reads as a preserved tool or a highly modified tow and show build.

quick_facts.Known for: Heavy-duty hauling, broad powertrain spread, strong Super Duty and Power Stroke following
quick_facts.Collector focus: OBS trucks, clean diesel examples, rare cab/bed combinations, and highly original workhorses

highlights: Powertrain and cab configuration matter enough that two F-250s of the same year can live in different markets.
Diesel, manual, and period-correct heavy-duty specs usually attract the strongest enthusiast attention.
Rust, towing wear, and suspension or drivetrain modifications change confidence quickly.
common_questions: Is this F-250 valuable because of diesel spec, era, or configuration?
How much hard work or towing history is hiding behind the current presentation?
Is the truck closer to original survivor, usable workhorse, or modified build?
buying_tips: Separate gas, diesel, and era-specific trucks before comping.
Check frame, cab corners, bed condition, and drivetrain documentation closely.
On modified trucks, decide whether the work broadens appeal or only fits a niche buyer.

honda:s2000.overview: The Honda S2000 market rewards originality, clean ownership stories, and exact spec details more than many other modern sports cars. AP1 versus AP2, color, mileage, modifications, and evidence of careful maintenance can move values sharply even among cars that look nearly identical at a glance.

quick_facts.Known for: High-revving naturally aspirated engines, precise shifter feel, lightweight roadster layout
quick_facts.Collector focus: AP1 vs AP2 distinctions, CR cars, low-mile originals, and clean stock color combinations

highlights: AP1 and AP2 cars are close enough to confuse casual buyers but different enough to matter materially in the market.
Stock-condition cars generally trade on a stronger curve than heavily modified ones.
Color, top condition, and service history often matter more than people expect in this segment.
common_questions: Is this the right AP1 or AP2 comp set for the car in front of me?
How stock is the suspension, exhaust, intake, and wheel setup?
Does the ownership history support collector-grade pricing or just enthusiast-driver pricing?
buying_tips: Separate AP1, AP2, and CR cars before drawing price conclusions.
Original hard-to-find parts, top condition, and alignment-friendly modifications matter a lot.
Favor documented valve-adjustment, fluid, and suspension maintenance over cosmetic freshness alone.

lexus:ls.overview: The Lexus LS market blends long-term durability, understated luxury, and trim-specific flagship appeal, with LS 400, LS 430, LS 460, and later hybrid or F Sport-adjacent cars behaving quite differently. Buyers pay up for condition, documentation, and unusually clean ownership stories more than flashy presentation.

quick_facts.Known for: Quiet flagship luxury, durability, smooth V8 ownership experience
quick_facts.Collector focus: Early LS 400s, preserved LS 430s, and exceptionally clean LS 460-era cars

highlights: The LS badge covers multiple very different flagship eras, so exact generation matters a lot.
Documentation and upkeep often matter more than modest mileage differences in this market.
Well-preserved colors, interior condition, and option packages can move demand faster than casual buyers expect.
common_questions: Which LS generation am I really comping against here?
Is the value story durability, flagship nostalgia, or unusual preservation?
How much deferred suspension, electronic, or interior work could still be hiding?
buying_tips: Comp LS 400, LS 430, LS 460, and later turbo/hybrid cars separately.
Prioritize service depth, interior preservation, and electronics health over headline mileage.
A clean boring-looking LS often trades better than a flashier neglected one.

lexus:lx.overview: The Lexus LX market sits at the intersection of luxury SUV demand and Land Cruiser durability, which makes generation, engine, and ownership story especially important. Buyers often shop these as long-life premium trucks, so condition and service depth can matter more than cosmetic freshness.

quick_facts.Known for: Land Cruiser underpinnings, luxury appointments, long-haul durability
quick_facts.Collector focus: LX 450 and LX 470 trucks, preserved examples, and high-spec low-mile later SUVs

highlights: The LX family behaves differently from ordinary luxury SUVs because buyers care about Land Cruiser credibility too.
Generation and engine choice materially change both demand and operating-cost expectations.
Clean rust-free trucks with strong maintenance records usually stand out quickly.
common_questions: Is this appealing as a luxury SUV, an overland-capable truck, or a durable collector-era cruiser?
How complete is the service history on suspension, drivetrain, and cooling systems?
Does the current price reflect genuine preservation or just Toyota-family reputation?
buying_tips: Comp LX 450, LX 470, and LX 570 trucks separately.
Rust, underbody condition, and suspension-system health matter more than glossy detailing.
Overland-style modifications can help one buyer and shrink the next buyer pool.

mini:cooper.overview: The MINI Cooper market mixes classic small-car charm with modern BMW-era hot-hatch personality, which means era, body style, and trim matter immediately. Buyers tend to separate classic Minis, standard Coopers, S models, wagons, and specialty variants into very different value bands.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact footprint, playful handling, iconic British design
quick_facts.Collector focus: Classic Minis, Cooper S variants, rare wagons, and unusually clean modern manuals

highlights: Classic Minis and modern BMW-era MINIs should be treated as different markets despite the shared name.
Body style and performance trim matter because buyers often shop for a very specific MINI personality.
Condition and documentation can outweigh mileage because the best buyer pool is picky about ownership stories.
common_questions: Is this a classic Mini, a modern Cooper, or a higher-spec Cooper S or JCW car?
How much of the value is tied to body style and rarity?
Does the current condition support enthusiast pricing or just design novelty?
buying_tips: Separate classic and modern cars before comping anything.
Look closely at trim level, gearbox, and maintenance because small spec differences matter here.
On classic cars, rust and restoration quality are still the first filters.

mitsubishi:montero.overview: The Mitsubishi Montero market rewards honest, rugged trucks with good documentation, especially as interest in capable 1990s and early-2000s SUVs keeps growing. Generation, trim, drivetrain, and whether the truck still feels original versus overbuilt for trails matter a lot.

quick_facts.Known for: Off-road credibility, Dakar heritage, durable global-spec SUV design
quick_facts.Collector focus: Gen 2 trucks, SR and LS trims, manuals, and preserved 4x4 examples

highlights: Second-generation Monteros currently draw the most enthusiast attention.
Trim and body style matter because short-wheelbase, manual, and nicer-spec trucks attract different buyers.
Off-road modifications can help utility value while still hurting collector originality.
common_questions: Is this Montero interesting because of generation, trim, or drivetrain?
How much of the truck is still close to factory versus trail-prepped?
Does the current value reflect rarity, condition, or just rising analog-SUV demand?
buying_tips: Separate Gen 1, Gen 2, and later trucks before comparing prices.
Look for drivetrain, cooling, rust, and suspension documentation before paying for apparent rarity.
Choose carefully between stock survivors and modified trail builds because buyers rarely treat them the same.

oldsmobile:cutlass.overview: The Oldsmobile Cutlass family covers personal-luxury coupes, sleeper V8 cars, wagons, and trim-heavy American survivors, so exact body style and era matter immediately. Condition, originality, and whether the car reads as malaise-era cruiser or muscle-adjacent collector piece drive value more than the badge alone.

quick_facts.Known for: Wide trim spread, strong 1970s coupe identity, durable cruiser appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: 442-related cars, Supreme coupes, clean wagons, and low-mile survivors

highlights: A Cutlass Supreme, Brougham sedan, and 442-adjacent car can live on very different value curves.
Low-mile originality often matters more than aggressive restoration in this buyer pool.
Body style and drivetrain condition can outweigh modest trim differences quickly.
common_questions: Is this car collectible because of trim, body style, or preservation?
How much of the drivetrain, paint, and interior are still period-correct?
Does the current ask reflect survivor value or just nostalgia?
buying_tips: Separate Supreme and other upscale trims from ordinary sedans before comping.
Inspect rust, vinyl top areas, interior trim, and diesel or V8 documentation closely.
On low-mile cars, originality usually matters more than cosmetic over-improvement.

saab:9-3.overview: The Saab 9-3 market mixes practical turbo ownership with niche enthusiast demand for Aeros, Viggens, convertibles, and wagons. Buyers care about exact drivetrain, body style, and maintenance depth because the best examples now trade on scarcity and ownership story as much as on specification.

quick_facts.Known for: Turbocharged character, quirky ergonomics, wagon and convertible niche appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: Aero cars, Viggen variants, SportCombis, and unusually clean manual examples

highlights: Body style matters a lot because convertibles and wagons attract different Saab buyers.
Manual Aero and Viggen-style cars can trade much differently from ordinary 2.0T drivers.
Documentation around maintenance and parts support matters because buyer confidence can be fragile.
common_questions: Is this 9-3 interesting because of Aero/Viggen hardware, body style, or just rarity?
How complete is the maintenance story on drivetrain, electrical, and top or wagon-specific issues?
Does the current price reflect a cult-car premium or simply a shrinking supply of clean Saabs?
buying_tips: Separate Aero, Viggen, convertibles, and wagons before comping.
Service depth and clean ownership history matter more than low mileage alone.
A well-kept boring Saab often trades better than a neglected rare-spec one.

volvo:v70.overview: The Volvo V70 market ranges from sensible wagons to genuinely quick R cars and rare enthusiast-spec examples, so drivetrain and trim matter immediately. Buyers value rarity, utility, and honest maintenance history, especially once V70R cars enter the mix.

quick_facts.Known for: Long-roof practicality, understated design, sleeper performance in R trims
quick_facts.Collector focus: V70R wagons, manuals, and unusually preserved enthusiast-spec cars

highlights: V70R cars trade on a very different curve from ordinary V70 wagons.
Condition and service history matter because complicated high-spec wagons can be expensive to sort.
Manuals and enthusiast-owner cars often stand out quickly in this niche buyer pool.
common_questions: Is this a normal V70, a lifted XC-style car, or a real V70R?
How much recent suspension, driveline, and electrical work has been handled?
Does the current ask reflect rarity, maintenance, or optimism?
buying_tips: Separate V70R and other high-spec wagons from ordinary V70 drivers.
Look for documentation on AWD, suspension, cooling, and transmission work before paying for rarity.
A clean well-documented wagon can deserve a real premium in this segment.

de-tomaso:pantera.overview: The De Tomaso Pantera market blends Italian design with Ford V8 simplicity, which is a big part of its enduring appeal. Buyers care a lot about provenance, rust and chassis quality, originality versus period modifications, and whether the car presents as a preserved exotic or a heavily reworked hot rod.

quick_facts.Known for: Italian styling, mid-engine layout, Ford V8 power, exotic-meets-muscle character
quick_facts.Collector focus: Early push-button-door cars, clean U.S.-market examples, and well-documented restorations

highlights: Panteras attract buyers who love the blend of exotic looks and more approachable Ford mechanicals.
Chassis condition, cooling upgrades, and the quality of any restoration work matter a lot.
Tasteful period-correct upgrades can help, but overbuilt restomods narrow the buyer pool.
common_questions: Is this Pantera still close to factory spec or more of a modified performance build?
How strong is the documentation around import history, restoration, and major mechanical work?
Does the current pricing reflect rarity, quality, or simply the badge and shape?
buying_tips: Inspect corrosion, panel fit, chassis repairs, and cooling-system history closely.
Separate cleaner original cars from heavily modified examples before comping.
Ford-engine familiarity helps upkeep, but the rest of the car still needs real exotic-car scrutiny.

ford:pantera.overview: Ford-badged Pantera coverage in Garage Radar is meant to catch the Ford-powered Pantera story that many buyers actually search for, even though the model's manufacturer history is more nuanced. The same value drivers still apply: build quality, rust, originality, and documentation matter more than badge phrasing alone.

quick_facts.Known for: Italian design with Ford V8 hardware and strong enthusiast search demand
quick_facts.Collector focus: Well-documented U.S.-market cars, clean restorations, and honest driver-grade Panteras

highlights: Many buyers search Panteras through their Ford connection, especially when thinking about parts and powertrain familiarity.
The core value story is still about Pantera condition and documentation, not just how the listing labels the make.
Cooling, chassis health, and restoration quality remain the first filters.
common_questions: Is this listing using Ford language because of the drivetrain or because of how the seller is positioning the car?
How complete is the documentation on provenance and major work?
Does the car present as collectible Pantera or just a loud modified exotic?
buying_tips: Use Pantera-specific comps regardless of whether the listing leans Ford or De Tomaso in its wording.
Focus on rust, cooling, and restoration quality before headline price.
Parts availability can be friendlier than other exotics, but buyer expectations are still very quality-sensitive.

tesla:model-3.overview: The Tesla Model 3 market is battery-, drivetrain-, and software-sensitive, with Long Range, Performance, and rear-drive cars behaving differently even when they look nearly identical. Buyers also care about feature generation, warranty position, and whether the car reads as efficient daily transport or a lightly used performance EV.

quick_facts.Known for: Mass-market EV adoption, quick performance variants, software-led ownership experience
quick_facts.Collector focus: Performance trims, low-mile early cars, and unusual color/spec combinations

highlights: Battery and drivetrain spec matter more than the shared Model 3 silhouette suggests.
Software-era changes and warranty confidence can shift value quickly.
Performance cars often attract a different buyer than range-focused daily drivers.
common_questions: Is this the exact battery and drivetrain combo buyers are chasing?
How much confidence is there around battery health, tires, and suspension wear?
Does the current pricing reflect EV depreciation, performance appeal, or low-mile condition?
buying_tips: Separate Performance, Long Range, and rear-drive cars before comping.
Look at charging hardware, warranty status, and recent service alongside cosmetics.
A newer weaker-spec car can trade against an older stronger-spec car in surprising ways, so be deliberate.

tesla:model-x.overview: The Tesla Model X market is unusual because falcon-wing-door drama, battery size, and performance spec all coexist with big-ticket complexity. Buyers care about drivetrain, seating layout, warranty and service history, and whether the car feels like a family luxury EV or a tech-heavy aging flagship.

quick_facts.Known for: Falcon-wing doors, high-performance EV SUV packaging, six- and seven-seat flexibility
quick_facts.Collector focus: P100D/Plaid halo cars and unusually clean lower-mile flagship specs

highlights: Battery and performance tier matter a lot, but so do door, trim, and suspension-condition stories.
The buyer pool is often balancing wow factor against long-term complexity.
Later better-supported specs can be more desirable than older headline-grabbing cars.
common_questions: Is this Model X compelling because of performance, seating, or novelty?
How strong is the warranty and service history story on high-cost systems?
Does the current price reflect flagship status or just rapid used-EV depreciation?
buying_tips: Separate early cars from later better-supported battery and interior revisions.
Look carefully at suspension, tires, door operation, and battery or drive-unit history.
A cheaper complex Model X can become expensive very quickly if service depth is thin.

tesla:model-y.overview: The Tesla Model Y market is broad and fast-moving, with Long Range and Performance trims forming the main value split. Buyers care about battery and drivetrain spec, software-era updates, and whether the car is priced as a practical family EV or as a quick premium crossover.

quick_facts.Known for: Popular crossover packaging, strong efficiency, familiar Tesla software ecosystem
quick_facts.Collector focus: Performance trims, unusual color/interior combinations, and very clean low-mile cars

highlights: Long Range and Performance cars trade differently enough that Model Y alone is too broad for pricing.
Minor-looking feature and build-era differences can matter when buyers compare newer examples.
This is a practical EV market first, but strong-spec cars still attract enthusiast interest.
common_questions: Is this the right battery and drivetrain spec for the ask?
How much of the value story is convenience and practicality versus outright performance?
Does the listing reflect current used-EV pricing or seller optimism?
buying_tips: Separate rear-drive, Long Range, and Performance cars before drawing price conclusions.
Look at warranty, charging setup, wheel/tire wear, and build-era details alongside mileage.
Small equipment differences can matter a lot when many comparable Model Ys are available.

toyota:4runner.overview: The Toyota 4Runner market remains strong because buyers trust the durability story, but values still split sharply by generation, drivetrain, rust exposure, and how stock or overland-modified a truck is. Clean documented examples often command a real premium over rougher trucks that look similar in photos.

quick_facts.Known for: Body-on-frame durability, strong resale, off-road and overland appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: Earlier generations, manuals, TRD-era trucks, and clean rust-free survivors

highlights: Generation and rust story matter a lot because the buyer pool is durability-focused.
Modified overland builds and stock trucks do not trade on the same curve.
Clean ownership and maintenance history can beat flashy accessories in this market.
common_questions: Is this 4Runner desirable because of generation, trim, or build style?
How much real off-road use is hiding behind the current presentation?
Does the value reflect Toyota reputation, condition, or true scarcity?
buying_tips: Check rust, underbody, frame, and suspension details before getting excited about accessories.
Separate stock trucks from lifted or overland-focused builds before comping.
Service history and honest condition usually matter more than a long list of mods.

toyota:land-cruiser.overview: The Toyota Land Cruiser market is unusually deep because buyers see these as both durable global trucks and collectible premium SUVs. Generation, engine, rust history, and whether the truck is preserved, restored, or overland-built matter a great deal.

quick_facts.Known for: Legendary durability, global-spec breadth, strong collector and expedition credibility
quick_facts.Collector focus: 40 Series trucks, 60/80/100 Series wagons, and unusually clean U.S.-market examples

highlights: Land Cruisers of different generations can behave like entirely different markets.
Rust, originality, and quality of restoration or expedition modifications drive value fast.
Buyers care about the truck's life story almost as much as the badge.
common_questions: Which Land Cruiser generation am I actually comparing here?
How much of the truck is preserved versus rebuilt or overland-modified?
Does the current price reflect genuine condition or just Land Cruiser reputation?
buying_tips: Use generation-aware comps before anything else.
Check rust, import history, drivetrain documentation, and restoration quality very closely.
Do not treat expedition builds and clean original trucks as interchangeable just because both are desirable.

toyota:supra.overview: The Toyota Supra market is still very trim- and generation-sensitive, with turbocharged A70 and A80 cars behaving very differently from later revival models. Buyers care deeply about drivetrain originality, transmission, modifications, and whether the car is a clean collector candidate or a tuner-era build.

quick_facts.Known for: Straight-six performance, turbo halo status, strong tuner and collector demand
quick_facts.Collector focus: A80 Twin Turbo manuals, clean A70 Turbos, and well-documented original cars

highlights: Turbo, transmission, and generation details matter so much that broad Supra comps are dangerous.
Modified cars can be exciting but often trade with a different buyer pool than stock survivors.
Documentation and originality still command a premium despite the model's tuning reputation.
common_questions: Am I looking at the exact Supra generation and turbo spec buyers really chase?
How original is the engine, turbo, transmission, and electronics setup?
Is the current ask driven by rarity, nostalgia, or a specific build quality story?
buying_tips: Separate naturally aspirated and turbo cars before comping.
Manuals, stock drivetrains, and clean documentation matter a lot here.
Treat heavily modified cars as their own market, not as automatic peers to stock survivors.

volkswagen:r32.overview: The Volkswagen R32 market sits in a sweet spot between practical hatchback usability and cult performance-car appeal. Buyers care about generation, transmission, originality, and whether the car still feels like a preserved enthusiast benchmark rather than a heavily modified hot hatch.

quick_facts.Known for: Narrow-angle VR6 soundtrack, AWD grip, understated hot-hatch packaging
quick_facts.Collector focus: Mk4 and Mk5 R32s, manual cars, low-mile originals, and clean unmodified examples

highlights: Transmission matters because manual and DSG cars can trade with slightly different buyers.
The best R32s stand out through originality, service history, and restrained modifications.
Buyers often treat the R32 as a cult benchmark rather than just another Golf.
common_questions: Is this the generation and transmission combo enthusiasts actually want?
How modified is the car compared with the stronger original examples?
Does the ask reflect rarity, condition, or nostalgia?
buying_tips: Separate Mk4 and Mk5 cars before comping.
Look for chain, AWD-system, suspension, and DSG or clutch documentation.
Tasteful period mods can be okay, but the cleanest stock cars usually attract the broadest demand.

volkswagen:westfalia.overview: Volkswagen Westfalia values are driven as much by camping configuration and restoration quality as by year alone. Buyers care about originality, rust, conversion correctness, and whether the van feels like an authentic usable camper or a cosmetic nostalgia project.

quick_facts.Known for: Camper conversion heritage, air-cooled nostalgia, road-trip utility
quick_facts.Collector focus: Split-window and Bay Window campers, preserved interiors, and high-quality restorations

highlights: Layout, camping equipment, and restoration quality can matter more than spec-sheet details.
Rust and conversion completeness are first-order value drivers in this market.
The best buyers are shopping for a full experience, not just a van with a famous name.
common_questions: Is this Westfalia authentically configured and complete?
How much restoration work is cosmetic versus structural and mechanical?
Does the current price reflect usable camping quality or just nostalgia?
buying_tips: Inspect rust, seams, camping equipment, and interior completeness before pricing anything else.
Separate true Westfalia campers from looser camper-style builds.
Mechanical simplicity helps, but poor restoration work can still make a cheap van very expensive.

volvo:s80.overview: The Volvo S80 market is a quieter one, but the best cars still stand out through powertrain choice, preservation, and honest flagship-sedan ownership stories. Buyers care about whether the car is a clean long-distance luxury sedan, a V8 oddball, or just a cheap commuter with deferred maintenance.

quick_facts.Known for: Understated comfort, safety-first flagship design, long-distance usability
quick_facts.Collector focus: V8 cars, rare specs, and unusually preserved low-mile examples

highlights: Powertrain is the biggest separator here because V8, T6, and 3.2 cars attract different buyers.
Preservation and maintenance depth matter more than mileage once these cars age into bargain territory.
The best cars are valued for honesty and condition, not for status.
common_questions: Is this S80 interesting because of engine, condition, or rarity?
How much deferred maintenance is hiding behind the current price?
Does the car feel like a usable flagship or a tired cheap luxury sedan?
buying_tips: Separate V8 and turbo cars from ordinary six-cylinder examples before comping.
Look carefully at electronics, cooling, suspension, and transmission history.
A clean documented S80 can deserve more respect than its badge might suggest.

volvo:xc70.overview: The Volvo XC70 market appeals to buyers who want wagon practicality, all-weather utility, and understated character. Values are driven by powertrain, maintenance depth, and whether the car still feels like a trustworthy long-haul wagon rather than an aging complexity trap.

quick_facts.Known for: Raised wagon practicality, AWD utility, understated all-road personality
quick_facts.Collector focus: Clean turbo examples, manuals, and unusually preserved later cars

highlights: The best XC70s win on condition and service history rather than on headline rarity.
AWD, suspension, and driveline upkeep matter more than cosmetics in this niche.
Buyers like the concept enough to pay for a clean example, but not enough to excuse neglect.
common_questions: Is this XC70 desirable because of drivetrain, preservation, or just wagon scarcity?
How complete is the story on AWD, suspension, and major maintenance?
Does the current ask reflect genuine condition or the idea of an all-road wagon?
buying_tips: Prioritize mechanical history and underbody condition over visual freshness.
Separate turbo and base drivetrains before comparing values.
A solid documented XC70 can be worth more than a cheaper wagon with hidden AWD or suspension issues.

acura:nsx.overview: The Acura NSX market is one of the clearest cases where engineering story, preservation, and spec discipline all matter at once. Buyers care about whether the car is an early analog coupe, a targa-style NSX-T, or a later refined car, and they pay close attention to originality, documented service, and how sympathetically the car has been treated.

quick_facts.Known for: All-aluminum construction, everyday-supercar usability, Ayrton Senna development lore
quick_facts.Collector focus: Early manuals, NSX-Ts, Zanardi cars, and highly original low-mile examples

highlights: Manual transmission, documented maintenance, and original body or paint story matter a lot here.
The market distinguishes clearly between preserved cars and modified tuner-era builds.
NSX-T and later-year cars can attract a different buyer than the earliest fixed-roof analog examples.
common_questions: Is this car interesting because of year, roof configuration, or its originality story?
How complete is the service history on timing belt, cooling, clutch, and suspension work?
Does the current price reflect rarity, preservation, or just the NSX badge?
buying_tips: Do not comp early coupes and later NSX-Ts without a deliberate adjustment.
Look closely at paint meter readings, VIN stickers, and service records before paying collector money.
Tasteful upgrades can work, but the strongest premiums still land on clean original cars.

audi:r8.overview: The Audi R8 market lives at the intersection of usable supercar and enthusiast-spec drivetrain shopping. Buyers care deeply about V8 versus V10, gated manual versus later dual-clutch cars, and whether the car presents as a preserved halo model or a heavily personalized exotics-era build.

quick_facts.Known for: Mid-engine layout, everyday drivability, gated-manual halo appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: Manual V8 cars, V10 Plus specs, low-mile coupes, and final-year examples

highlights: Transmission and engine are the biggest separators because manual V8 cars and later V10s do not trade on the same curve.
Condition, carbon package, magnetic-ride history, and clean ownership matter a lot once prices move into collector territory.
The market rewards preserved, well-documented cars more than flashy modifications.
common_questions: Is this the drivetrain and transmission combo enthusiasts actually chase?
How complete is the service story on clutch, suspension, brakes, and cosmetic condition?
Does the current price reflect rarity, spec, or just supercar theater?
buying_tips: Separate manual V8, R-tronic, S tronic, and V10 cars before drawing price conclusions.
Check for cosmetic honesty and expensive deferred maintenance, not just low miles.
A clean understated R8 often trades better than a louder heavily customized one.

audi:tt.overview: The Audi TT market ranges from affordable style-forward coupes to genuinely interesting enthusiast variants, so the exact drivetrain and generation matter more than the shape alone suggests. Buyers care about whether the car is a base cruiser, a 3.2 Quattro oddball, a TT RS, or a preserved early-design icon.

quick_facts.Known for: Design-led compact sports-car packaging, Quattro variants, strong enthusiast subculture
quick_facts.Collector focus: Mk1 design icons, 3.2 Quattro cars, TT RS models, and manual examples

highlights: The market separates ordinary TT drivers from the more special 3.2, TTS, and TT RS cars quickly.
Generation, transmission, and whether the car is still tastefully preserved all influence demand.
Manual cars and unusual performance specs can punch above the broader TT market.
common_questions: Is this just a stylish TT, or one of the enthusiast versions buyers really hunt?
How much of the current value story comes from design, drivetrain, or rarity?
Is the car preserved enough to read as collectible rather than disposable?
buying_tips: Separate base TT, 3.2, TTS, and TT RS cars before comping.
Check Haldex, timing, suspension, and DSG or clutch history where relevant.
Tasteful originality matters more here than long mod lists.

bmw:m5.overview: The BMW M5 is one of those nameplates where every generation carries its own mythology, and the market prices them that way. Buyers care about engine character, transmission, service story, and whether the car is a preserved super-sedan benchmark or a fast-depreciating maintenance project.

quick_facts.Known for: Executive-sedan packaging with serious performance, generation-defining engines, autobahn image
quick_facts.Collector focus: E28, E34, E39, V10 E60 manuals, special editions, and clean later Competition cars

highlights: Each M5 generation feels like its own sub-market because the engine and transmission story changes so much.
Deferred maintenance can destroy a cheap-looking deal very quickly in this segment.
The best values usually sit where originality, documentation, and usable condition still line up.
common_questions: Which M5 generation am I actually comparing here?
How much of the value story is preserved originality versus scary maintenance deferred?
Does the current price reflect special spec, special condition, or just badge appeal?
buying_tips: Use generation-aware comps before almost anything else.
Read the maintenance history like it is part of the asking price, because it is.
Manuals, special editions, and very clean stock cars deserve their own comp set.

bmw:z3.overview: The BMW Z3 market splits between cheerful entry-level roadsters, six-cylinder sweet spots, and the much more serious M Roadster and M Coupe cars. Buyers care about engine, body style, originality, and whether the car feels like a preserved analog BMW or a cheap used convertible with hidden needs.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact roadster packaging, long-hood proportions, M Coupe cult following
quick_facts.Collector focus: Z3 M Roadster, M Coupe, late six-cylinder manuals, and clean low-mile cars

highlights: Roadster and coupe values can diverge sharply because the buyer pools are different.
Engine size and transmission matter more than the shared Z3 silhouette suggests.
Clean honest analog examples tend to stand out in a market full of lightly neglected convertibles.
common_questions: Is this Z3 desirable because of engine, body style, or rarity?
How much deferred maintenance is hiding beneath a simple-looking roadster?
Does the current price reflect collector-spec M desirability or just summer-car appeal?
buying_tips: Separate four-cylinder, six-cylinder, and M cars before comping.
Body style matters enough that roadsters and coupes should not be blended casually.
Look for cooling-system, suspension, and interior-condition honesty before paying up.

chevrolet:camaro.overview: The Chevrolet Camaro market is broad, but the real money moves around engine, era, and whether the car is a mainstream trim or a halo variant. Buyers care about authenticity, drivetrain, documentation, and whether the car reads as preserved muscle, modern performance, or a rough project dressed up with badges.

quick_facts.Known for: Pony-car rivalry, deep V8 hierarchy, strong special-edition ecosystem
quick_facts.Collector focus: SS, Z/28, Yenko-linked history, ZL1, 1LE, and unusually clean analog-era cars

highlights: Small trim differences matter a lot because Camaro buyers often shop a very specific powertrain and package.
Documentation and authenticity become critical once the car is presented as something special.
The market treats classic muscle, 1980s/1990s nostalgia cars, and modern super-coupes very differently.
common_questions: Is this the exact engine and trim package buyers actually pay up for?
How original is the drivetrain, body, and badge story?
Does the current ask reflect real spec quality or just Camaro optimism?
buying_tips: Separate base, SS, Z/28, and ZL1/1LE-style cars before comping.
Verify trim and engine documentation before trusting what the emblems say.
Condition and provenance matter more than loud aftermarket modifications.

jeep:wrangler.overview: The Jeep Wrangler market is one of the clearest examples of stock versus build-story value. Buyers care about generation, door count, Rubicon or special-edition content, and whether the Jeep reads as a clean usable truck or a hard-used off-road build with unknown compromises.

quick_facts.Known for: Open-top off-road utility, huge aftermarket, strong identity-driven buyer base
quick_facts.Collector focus: TJ and LJ manuals, Rubicons, clean special editions, and unusually stock survivors

highlights: Stock Wranglers and heavily modified off-road builds do not trade on the same curve.
Door count, axle spec, and Rubicon content can matter as much as age.
Buyers often pay up for honest, un-abused Jeeps with a clean story.
common_questions: Is this Wrangler desirable because of trim, generation, or build quality?
How much real trail use or hidden damage is sitting behind the accessories?
Does the current pricing reflect stock desirability or mod-list enthusiasm?
buying_tips: Separate stock, mildly upgraded, and serious trail builds before comping.
Look hard at rust, underbody damage, steering, suspension, and tire-size side effects.
A clean ordinary Wrangler can trade better than a louder Jeep with a murkier history.

lexus:is.overview: The Lexus IS market ranges from sensible compact sports sedans to genuinely special enthusiast trims, and the buyer pool changes with each step. Buyers care about whether the car is a straightforward IS 300/350, a manual-era analog favorite, or an IS F-style halo car with a different value story altogether.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact rear-drive luxury-sport positioning, Toyota reliability halo, sleeper performance variants
quick_facts.Collector focus: Manual IS 300s, clean IS F cars, and unusually preserved early generations

highlights: The enthusiast market pays for IS 300 manuals and IS Fs very differently from ordinary commuter-spec cars.
Condition and tasteful originality matter because many early IS cars were modified hard.
Reliable-sports-sedan reputation supports the floor, but the real premiums still depend on spec.
common_questions: Is this just an IS sedan, or one of the enthusiast versions buyers really chase?
How original is the car versus tuner-era modifications and cosmetic changes?
Does the current ask reflect rarity, preservation, or just brand trust?
buying_tips: Separate IS 300, IS 350, and IS F cars before comping.
Manuals and clean early cars deserve extra scrutiny because they attract the strongest demand.
A tidy stock IS often trades better than a louder but less coherent modified one.

lexus:lc.overview: The Lexus LC market is still young, but buyers already shop it like a design-led grand tourer where drivetrain and spec matter a lot. The split between LC 500 and LC 500h, plus color and option choices, shapes desirability more than the shared bodywork suggests.

quick_facts.Known for: Dramatic GT styling, naturally aspirated V8 appeal, luxury-coupe craftsmanship
quick_facts.Collector focus: LC 500 V8 cars, Inspiration Series specs, convertibles, and unusual color combinations

highlights: The V8 LC 500 and hybrid 500h can attract very different buyers.
Color, wheel, and interior combinations matter because this is a design-led purchase.
Low-volume GT status gives clean highly specced cars a stronger story than ordinary used-luxury comps would suggest.
common_questions: Is this the V8 grand-touring spec buyers really want, or the efficiency-oriented hybrid?
How much of the value story is driven by spec, color, and presentation?
Does the current ask reflect rarity and desirability, or just low production volume?
buying_tips: Separate LC 500 and LC 500h cars before comping.
Watch the option sheet because the best colors and interiors can materially help demand.
A well-kept striking-spec LC can deserve more respect than broader used-luxury pricing implies.

mercedes-benz:sl-class.overview: The Mercedes-Benz SL-Class market spans elegant cruisers, hardtop convertibles, and genuine AMG halo cars, so broad SL pricing is rarely enough on its own. Buyers care about era, engine, roof type, documentation, and whether the car presents as a usable classic or a complex deferred-maintenance luxury toy.

quick_facts.Known for: Luxury roadster lineage, long model history, broad spread from boulevard cruiser to AMG missile
quick_facts.Collector focus: Pagoda cars, R107 450/560 SLs, SL55 AMG, SL63 AMG, and pristine low-mile examples

highlights: A 450 SL and an SL63 AMG share a badge but not a market, so generation and powertrain are everything.
Preservation, service history, and roof or top-condition story matter heavily on older SLs.
The strongest buyers are usually shopping for a very specific era and use case.
common_questions: Which SL era am I really comparing against here?
Is the value story luxury cruising, classic collecting, or AMG performance?
How much of the current price depends on condition and maintenance confidence?
buying_tips: Comp R107, R129, and later AMG cars separately.
Service history, cosmetics, and top mechanism condition matter more than the badge alone.
A clean ordinary SL can trade much better than a rougher 'special' one.

subaru:wrx.overview: The Subaru WRX market lives on a knife edge between collector nostalgia and modified-driver reality. Buyers care about generation, STI versus non-STI, documentation, and whether the car still feels like a coherent enthusiast example rather than a hard-used build with too many unknowns.

quick_facts.Known for: Turbocharged AWD performance, rally image, huge tuning culture
quick_facts.Collector focus: GC and GD cars, clean WRX STI examples, wagons, and unusually stock survivors

highlights: Stock versus modified is one of the biggest value splits anywhere in the sport-compact market.
Generation and body style matter because sedan, wagon, and hatch buyers are not always interchangeable.
The strongest premiums land on clean documented cars that escaped the rougher tuner pipeline.
common_questions: Is this WRX desirable because of generation, body style, or STI content?
How much modification and hard use is hiding behind the current presentation?
Does the current ask reflect collector-grade preservation or just turbo-AWD hype?
buying_tips: Separate WRX and WRX STI cars before comping.
Compression, tuning, drivetrain, and rust history matter more than cosmetic freshness.
A clean stock-ish car is usually easier to value than a big-power build.

toyota:fj-cruiser.overview: The Toyota FJ Cruiser market stays strong because buyers see it as both durable off-road hardware and a retro-styled collectible modern Toyota. Values depend on mileage, rust, modification intensity, and whether the truck still reads as a clean honest example rather than a hard-used trail build.

quick_facts.Known for: Retro design, strong off-road credibility, robust resale support
quick_facts.Collector focus: Trail Teams editions, manuals, final-year trucks, and unusually clean stock examples

highlights: Low-mile clean trucks and special editions attract a different buyer than modified trail rigs.
Rust, underbody condition, and real off-road use matter more than cosmetic shine.
Toyota durability supports values, but the best premiums still go to well-kept honest trucks.
common_questions: Is this FJ desirable because of rarity, condition, or its off-road build story?
How much real trail use or hidden corrosion is sitting behind the current look?
Does the current ask reflect clean modern-Toyota scarcity or just seller enthusiasm?
buying_tips: Separate stock trucks from lifted or heavily accessorized builds before comping.
Check underbody, frame, suspension, and evidence of off-road wear carefully.
Special editions and manuals deserve their own comp set when the condition supports it.

audi:a6.overview: The Audi A6 market blends understated executive-car appeal with a surprising amount of drivetrain and body-style complexity. Buyers care about whether the car is a Quattro sedan, an Allroad-style wagon, a higher-performance derivative, or just a comfortable luxury daily with deferred maintenance risk.

quick_facts.Known for: Quattro all-weather usability, discreet executive design, broad engine and trim spread
quick_facts.Collector focus: Manual wagons, Allroads, unusual clean V8-era cars, and halo-performance derivatives

highlights: Sedan and wagon buyers often value these cars differently, especially once Allroad or performance variants enter the picture.
Maintenance history matters more than low sticker pricing because these can become expensive quietly.
The cleanest examples win by being coherent, well-kept, and correctly specced rather than flashy.
common_questions: Is this an ordinary A6, an Allroad-style car, or a true performance derivative?
How complete is the service history on suspension, cooling, and drivetrain systems?
Does the current ask reflect rarity, spec, or just low-entry used-luxury pricing?
buying_tips: Separate sedan, wagon, and Allroad-style cars before comping.
Quattro hardware, suspension, and electronics history should move your confidence quickly.
A cleaner better-documented A6 often trades better than a cheaper higher-spec project.

audi:a8.overview: The Audi A8 market is all about flagship spec and ownership discipline. Buyers care about long-wheelbase versus standard cars, V8 or W12-era prestige, and whether the car presents as a genuinely preserved luxury flagship or just a very expensive-to-own bargain.

quick_facts.Known for: Aluminum-intensive flagship engineering, understated luxury, Quattro traction
quick_facts.Collector focus: W12 and S8-era halo cars, clean low-mile long-wheelbase examples, and strong preservation stories

highlights: These cars can look similar while carrying very different luxury and performance stories underneath.
Deferred maintenance is the main value trap in the A8 market, not cosmetic presentation.
Well-kept flagship Audis appeal to informed buyers who shop spec and service history closely.
common_questions: Is this interesting because of flagship comfort, performance spec, or unusual drivetrain?
How much of the value story depends on maintenance confidence rather than mileage?
Does the current ask reflect preservation quality or just big-car depreciation?
buying_tips: Separate A8, A8L, and S8-style cars before comping.
Read the service history like it is part of the option sheet, because ownership cost is central here.
A tidy well-documented A8 can deserve more respect than a cheaper flashier one.

audi:q7.overview: The Audi Q7 market lives between practical premium-family SUV duty and understated German-spec shopping. Buyers care about engine, row configuration, maintenance depth, and whether the car still feels like a trustworthy luxury hauler rather than a complexity-heavy used bargain.

quick_facts.Known for: Quattro utility, three-row packaging, broad premium-SUV appeal
quick_facts.Collector focus: Low-mile higher-output trims, well-kept TDI/3.0T examples, and unusual spec combinations

highlights: Engine choice matters because buyers view TDI, 3.0T, and hotter trims through different ownership lenses.
Service history and suspension or electronics health shape value more than the broad Q7 badge.
The best Q7s sell on cleanliness and complete stories, not just luxury features.
common_questions: Is the appeal here family utility, unusual drivetrain, or premium spec?
How much maintenance risk is hidden behind the current price?
Does the current ask reflect condition and equipment or just a premium badge?
buying_tips: Separate diesel, gasoline, and more performance-oriented trims before comping.
Look at service history for suspension, cooling, electronics, and drivetrain systems.
A cleaner simpler Q7 can trade better than a more loaded but shakier one.

audi:rs6.overview: The Audi RS6 market is a narrower enthusiast one where body style, performance pedigree, and preservation all matter. Buyers pay for the idea of a super-wagon or super-sedan done right, but they still care deeply about maintenance, modifications, and whether the car feels factory-coherent.

quick_facts.Known for: Extreme performance in practical body styles, twin-turbo punch, understated menace
quick_facts.Collector focus: Avant bodies, rare generations, low-mile cars, and clean unmodified examples

highlights: The RS6 buyer is usually spec-aware enough that body style and generation matter immediately.
Modification risk and service history can change buyer confidence faster than headline horsepower.
A clean practical halo car has a stronger story than a rougher one with a louder setup.
common_questions: Is this the exact RS6 generation and body style buyers chase?
How close is the car to factory-correct versus aggressively modified?
Does the current ask reflect true desirability or expensive maintenance lurking nearby?
buying_tips: Separate generations and body styles before comping.
Brake, suspension, turbo, and electronics history matter a lot in this market.
The cleanest lightly used RS6s attract a broader buyer pool than heavily tuned ones.

audi:s5.overview: The Audi S5 market is where design, drivetrain, and body style all tug on value at once. Coupe and cabriolet buyers can value the same badge very differently, and the strongest cars are usually the ones that feel crisp, correctly specced, and properly maintained rather than merely flashy.

quick_facts.Known for: Elegant sporty design, Quattro grip, usable premium-performance balance
quick_facts.Collector focus: Manual coupes, V8-era cars, unusual color combinations, and preserved examples

highlights: Body style matters because coupes, cabriolets, and Sportbacks appeal to different buyers.
Generation and engine change the character enough that broad S5 pricing can be misleading.
The best S5s win on spec coherence, maintenance, and clean presentation.
common_questions: Is this the body style and engine combo buyers really want?
How complete is the service story on supercharger, clutch or DSG, and suspension systems?
Does the current price reflect style appeal, rarity, or actual preservation?
buying_tips: Separate coupe, cabriolet, and Sportback cars before comping.
Manuals and earlier V8 cars deserve different comparisons from later 3.0T examples.
A clean understated S5 usually sells better than a louder cosmetically modified one.

audi:s8.overview: The Audi S8 occupies a special niche as a stealth flagship performance sedan. Buyers care about drivetrain, generation, and whether the car feels like a preserved executive missile or a very fast example of luxury-car depreciation.

quick_facts.Known for: Flagship comfort with serious performance, understated image, Quattro stability
quick_facts.Collector focus: D2 and D3-era cars, V10/V8 flagships, clean low-mile examples, and strong service stories

highlights: The S8 buyer is usually shopping a very specific generation and engine story.
Preservation and maintenance confidence matter more than the S8 badge alone.
These cars can feel like bargains until neglected flagship complexity shows up.
common_questions: Is this appealing because of generation, engine, or rarity?
How much deferred maintenance risk is hidden beneath the current ask?
Does the value reflect true halo-sedan appeal or simple luxury-car depreciation?
buying_tips: Separate S8 generations before comping.
Look closely at drivetrain, suspension, and electronics history.
A documented well-kept S8 can deserve a premium over a cheaper but shakier car.

lexus:gs.overview: The Lexus GS market is where quiet premium-sedan durability meets occasional enthusiast demand. Buyers care about whether the car is a regular GS, a V8 GS 430/460 style car, or a cleaner performance-leaning spec, and values respond strongly to preservation and maintenance honesty.

quick_facts.Known for: Understated luxury-sedan reliability, rear-drive dynamics, broad long-term usability
quick_facts.Collector focus: GS 300/400 analog-era cars, GS 350 sport sedans, and especially preserved low-mile examples

highlights: The GS market rewards clean preserved cars because so many lived ordinary commuter lives.
Engine and generation matter more than the quiet styling suggests.
Buyer confidence grows quickly when a GS feels honest, complete, and not neglected.
common_questions: Is this GS interesting because of drivetrain, generation, or simply condition?
How complete is the service story on a car that may have been used daily for years?
Does the current ask reflect genuine preservation or just Lexus reliability reputation?
buying_tips: Separate six-cylinder and V8 GS cars before comping.
Service records, interior condition, and clean ownership matter a lot on quiet executive sedans like this.
A very honest GS can deserve more respect than the badge alone implies.

lexus:sc.overview: The Lexus SC market is a niche one where comfort, style, and drivetrain character all shape value. Buyers care about whether the car is a smooth V8 grand-touring coupe or convertible and whether it still feels like a preserved luxury cruiser rather than a cheap used toy.

quick_facts.Known for: Smooth V8 or six-cylinder luxury touring, understated style, long-term durability
quick_facts.Collector focus: SC 300 manuals, SC 430 convertibles, and unusually preserved low-mile examples

highlights: The SC story changes a lot between the earlier coupes and the later SC 430 convertible.
Condition and preservation matter more than rarity alone because buyers want the ownership experience to feel special.
Clean low-mile examples can stand apart in a market full of tired luxury cruisers.
common_questions: Is this SC desirable because of engine, body style, or preservation?
How much deferred maintenance is hiding beneath a refined-looking exterior?
Does the current ask reflect true GT appeal or just used-luxury softness?

Interior condition, top or glass-roof hardware, and service depth matter a lot here.
The best SCs sell on complete presentation, not just Lexus reliability.

mercedes-benz:c-class.overview: The Mercedes-Benz C-Class spans everything from sensible compact luxury sedans to AMG performance cars, so the family is much broader than the badge makes it look. Buyers care about engine, body style, and whether the car is just an affordable used Mercedes or a genuinely interesting enthusiast spec.

quick_facts.Known for: Compact executive-car positioning, broad drivetrain spread, AMG halo variants
quick_facts.Collector focus: Manual oddballs, AMG C-Class cars, wagons, and preserved analog-era examples

highlights: Mainstream four-cylinder C-Class cars and AMG versions belong to very different markets.
Preservation, service depth, and body style determine whether the car reads as special or disposable.
Quietly clean C-Class examples can punch above what buyers expect from the badge.
common_questions: Is this just a regular C-Class, or one of the variants buyers actually chase?
How much maintenance confidence is there behind the current price?
Does the car sell on rarity, AMG cachet, or simply condition?

Body style matters more than it seems once wagons, coupes, or cabriolets show up.
A tidy documented C-Class is much easier to value than a cheaper neglected one.

mercedes-benz:cl-class.overview: The Mercedes-Benz CL-Class is a niche flagship-coupe market where luxury, power, and complexity all meet. Buyers care about whether the car is a smooth V8 grand tourer or a serious AMG coupe and whether the ownership story supports the badge prestige.

quick_facts.Collector focus: CL55 AMG, CL65 AMG, low-mile preserved coupes, and unusual high-spec examples
